title: Second Year
prose: The castle feels different the second time — smaller, somehow, in the way a place always shrinks once you've learned its shortcuts. You know which staircases to avoid at rush hour now, which portraits gossip, which corridor smells permanently of dungbombs.
There's a new crop of first-years at the Sorting, gawping up at the enchanted ceiling exactly as you did a year ago, and it's strange to realize you're not the newest thing in the castle anymore.

choices.label: Head to your first lesson of the year.
